title: Cancel a Subscription
summary: End a recurring subscription service without hassle.
learnerPromise: You will be able to cancel an unwanted subscription and confirm it will not bill you again.
whyItMatters: Unused subscriptions drain money every month, and knowing how to cancel them quickly helps you keep more of your money for things you actually use.
realLifeUses: Ending a streaming service you finished watching
Canceling a free trial before it converts to a paid plan
Stopping a monthly app subscription you no longer need
Cutting back on expenses by removing duplicate services
result: You cancel one subscription and keep proof of cancellation.
youWillLearn: Find the cancellation option in account or billing settings
Follow the cancellation steps even when the service tries to keep you
Confirm the cancellation with a screenshot or email
Check your bank account to make sure no further charges appear
miniChallenge: Cancel one subscription you are not actively using and take a screenshot of the confirmation.
steps: Log into the subscription service on its website or app.
Go to account settings, billing, or membership and look for cancel or manage plan.
Follow the cancellation steps carefully; some services hide this behind multiple screens.
Save the confirmation screen, confirmation email, or cancellation number as proof.
Check your payment method in a few days to make sure no new charge appeared.
completionCriteria: You found and used the cancellation flow for at least one subscription
You saved proof that the cancellation was completed
commonProblems: Services that make you call instead of canceling online
Offers of a discounted rate to tempt you to stay
Cancellation buttons hidden under layers of menus
Annual plans that may not offer a refund for remaining months
tips: If a service makes cancellation difficult, payment apps like PayPal or your bank may let you stop future payments directly
Some services stay active until the end of the current billing period even after you cancel
Set a calendar reminder to check your bank statement after the next billing date
